items.docs: Checks whether the Split Evaluator service is running. If the service is running, it responds with the text pong and HTTP status code 200.
Returns the current version of the Split Evaluator service.

Checks that the Split Evaluator is connected to Split servers and ready to process evaluation requests. Returns 200 if everything is working as expected or 500 if there is an issue, along with a message explaining the status.

info.name: Get treatment for a feature flag

params.name: key
params.description: The customer key to evaluate the feature flag against

params.name: split-name
params.description: The name of the feature flag to evaluate

params.name: bucketing-key
params.description: Optional bucketing key used for consistent percentage-based treatment assignments

params.name: attributes
params.description: JSON string of attributes used in targeting rule evaluation

params.name: impressions-disabled
params.description: When set to true, disables impression logging for this evaluation

items.docs: Evaluates a single feature flag for the given key and returns the treatment value. Optionally includes dynamic configuration associated with the treatment. This is the primary endpoint for feature flag evaluation.

params.name: key
params.description: The customer key to evaluate the feature flag against

params.name: split-name
params.description: The name of the feature flag to evaluate

params.name: bucketing-key
params.description: Optional bucketing key for consistent assignments

items.docs: Evaluates a single feature flag for the given key with attributes passed in the request body. This is preferred over the GET method when attributes contain complex or large data structures.

info.name: Get treatments for multiple feature flags

params.name: key
params.description: The customer key to evaluate feature flags against

params.name: split-names
params.description: Comma-separated list of feature flag names to evaluate

params.name: bucketing-key
params.description: Optional bucketing key for consistent assignments

params.name: attributes
params.description: JSON string of attributes for targeting rule evaluation

params.name: impressions-disabled
params.description: When set to true, disables impression logging for these evaluations

items.docs: Evaluates multiple feature flags for the given key in a single request and returns the treatment values for each. More efficient than making individual get-treatment calls.

params.name: key
params.description: The customer key to evaluate feature flags against

params.name: split-names
params.description: Comma-separated list of feature flag names to evaluate

params.name: bucketing-key
params.description: Optional bucketing key for consistent assignments

items.docs: Evaluates multiple feature flags for the given key with attributes passed in the request body.

info.name: Get all treatments for a key

params.name: keys
params.description: JSON string containing an array of key objects with matchingKey and trafficType properties, and optionally a bucketingKey

params.name: attributes
params.description: JSON string of attributes for targeting rule evaluation

params.name: impressions-disabled
params.description: When set to true, disables impression logging for these evaluations

items.docs: Evaluates all available feature flags for the given keys and traffic types and returns the treatment values. Useful for retrieving a complete set of flag evaluations for a user session.

params.name: keys
params.description: JSON string containing an array of key objects

items.docs: Evaluates all available feature flags for the given keys with attributes passed in the request body.

params.name: key
params.description: The customer key associated with the event

params.name: traffic-type
params.description: The traffic type for the event

params.name: event-type
params.description: The type of event being tracked (e.g., purchase, click, signup)

params.name: value
params.description: Optional numeric value associated with the event

params.name: properties
params.description: JSON string with up to 15 key-value pairs of event properties. Values must be boolean, string, number, or null.

items.docs: Records a custom event for the given key and event type. Events are used to measure the impact of feature flags on user actions and experimentation metrics.

params.name: key
params.description: The customer key associated with the event

params.name: traffic-type
params.description: The traffic type for the event

params.name: event-type
params.description: The type of event being tracked

params.name: value
params.description: Optional numeric value associated with the event

items.docs: Records a custom event with properties passed in the request body. Preferred for events with complex property structures.
